channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Florida congresswoman wants Trump's face to be carved on Mount Rushmore
item.description: The Mount Rushmore National Memorial in South Dakota currently features the 60-foot-tall heads of four U.S. presidents.
item.dc:creator: Sierra Rains, NewsNation

item.title: Ousted IG: Trump firings a 'a potentially existential threat' to government oversight
item.description: (The Hill) -- One of 17 government watchdogs ousted by President Trump said that the move was a “a potentially existential threat” to a crucial government oversight tool.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, KRON4

item.title: What Trump's funding freeze could mean for universities, nonprofits and more
item.description: A White House directive to pause federal grants and loans has raised uncertainty for many universities, states and nonprofit organizations.
item.dc:creator: David A. Lieb, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: 2 Tampa Bay area restaurants make list of most romantic in the US, according to OpenTable
item.description: (NEXSTAR) - Valentine's Day is just around the corner and finding the perfect restaurant to take the one you love can always be challenging.
item.dc:creator: Nick Jachim, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Mica Miller's death deeply shook Solid Rock church, John-Paul Miller docs show
item.description: Sam Rickman is among a dozen people at the center of legal action by John-Paul Miller on harassment complaints, according to court records
item.dc:creator: Adam Benson, NewsNation

item.title: How Donald Trump and Project 2025 previewed the federal grant freeze
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — A White House order to freeze federal grants reflects a theory of presidential power that Donald Trump clearly endorsed during his 2024 campaign.
item.dc:creator: BILL BARROW, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Bryan Kohberger case: Idaho judge to unseal transcript of closed-door hearing
item.description: A transcript from Bryan Kohberger's closed-door hearing on investigative genetic genealogy evidence held last week will be made public, in part, judge says.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Senate confirms Sean Duffy to lead Department of Transportation
item.description: The Senate on Tuesday confirmed former Rep. Sean Duffy (R-Wis.) to be the next Transportation secretary, putting him in place to lead a sprawling agency that oversees air travel, highways, pipelines and railroads.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: Sean Duffy is confirmed by the Senate to lead the Transportation Department
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Sean Duffy was confirmed Tuesday as transportation secretary, giving him a key role in helping President Donald Trump cut regulations and fix the nation's infrastructure. The former Wisconsin congressman has promised safer Boeing planes, less regulation and help for U.S.
item.dc:creator: BERNARD CONDON, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Pence group uses Trump's criticism against RFK Jr. in new ad
item.description: An advocacy group founded by former Vice President Mike Pence is escalating its fight against Robert F. Kennedy Jr.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: ICE snags criminal alien paroled 17 years into life sentence for pregnant woman's murder
item.description: Cesar Polanco, a criminal alien from the Dominican Republic who killed his pregnant girlfriend, has been taken into ICE custody after being granted parole in MA.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Killer mom Susan Smith is now 'complete nightmare' after parole was denied: report
item.description: South Carolina killer mom Susan Smith, who drowned her two sons, is reportedly acting like a "complete nightmare" behind bars, months after she was denied parole.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Coca-Cola introduces new flavor
item.description: A year after the launch of the short-lived Coca-Cola Spiced, Coke is adding another new flavor to its lineup. Coca-Cola Orange Cream is scheduled to go on sale Feb. 10 in the U.S. and Canada. It will be sold in regular and zero-sugar varieties. Atlanta-based Coca-Cola Co.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Hispanic community reacts to ICE operations in Austin
item.description: In a part of Austin with deep Latino roots, long-time Hispanic community leader Paul Saldana said he feels for his community.
item.dc:creator: Erica Pauda, NewsNation

item.title: Gary Peters will not run for reelection, opening key Senate seat
item.description: The 66-year-old’s unexpected decision Tuesday to step aside after just two terms comes as a surprise.
item.dc:creator: JOEY CAPPELLETTI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: American Airlines worker struck, killed by vehicle at Charlotte Airport
item.description: A worker at Charlotte Douglas International Airport (CLT) died Monday after being struck by an airline ramp vehicle on the tarmac, local police have confirmed.
item.dc:creator: Abby Mittower, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Additional Texas National Guard troops arrive at border
item.description: Over 400 Texas National Guard troops and equipment began arriving in the Rio Grande Valley on Monday evening to assist with border security.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, Fox 5

item.title: Video shows 'shopping cart killer' suspect leaving hotel room with women's bodies: prosecutors
item.description: A defendant dubbed by police as the “shopping cart killer" is on trial in Virginia accused of slaying two women in 2021 having met them on social media dating apps and taking them to his hotel room.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hawaii's Kilauea volcano is spewing lava once again
item.description: HONOLULU (AP) — One of the world’s most active volcanoes is back in action. The Kilauea volcano at Hawaii Volcanoes National Park is spewing lava once again, the seventh recorded episode in recent weeks. The eruption that began Dec. 23 in a crater at Kilauea's summit has paused periodically.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Blake Lively and Justin Baldoni get March 2026 trial date for her 'It Ends With Us' lawsuit
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A New York judge set a March 2026 trial date on Monday and moved an initial conference from mid-February to next week as the public feud between Blake Lively and her “It Ends With Us” costar and director Justin Baldoni continued to grow and accelerate.
item.dc:creator: LARRY NEUMEISTER and ANDREW DALTON, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Family, friends of Gabby Petito share details surrounding her death in upcoming Netflix documentary
item.description: An upcoming Netflix documentary will shed new light on the story of Gabby Petito and her death at the hands of her fiancé, Brian Laundrie.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Rodriguez, WGN 9

item.title: Family, friends of Gabby Petito share details surrounding her death in upcoming Netflix documentary
item.description: An upcoming Netflix documentary will shed new light on the story of Gabby Petito and her death at the hands of her fiancé, Brian Laundrie.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Rodriguez, ABC 4

item.title: Full-scale replica of Anne Frank's hidden annex opens in NYC
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A full-scale replica of the secret annex where Anne Frank penned her famous diary opened in New York City on Monday as the world marked International Holocaust Remembrance Day.
item.dc:creator: PHILIP MARCELO and JOSEPH FREDERICK, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Rain douses wildfires in Southern California without causing serious mudslides
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — The first significant storm of the season brought snow and downpours to Southern California that doused wildfires and caused some ash and mud to flow across streets in the Los Angeles area on Monday.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER WEBER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump addresses House Republicans at Florida retreat: 5 takeaways
item.description: DORAL, Fla. — President Trump floated running for a third term, commented on a new Chinese artificial intelligence app, and urged House Republicans to unify and quickly advance his legislative priorities in a speech to the lawmakers at their annual policy retreat.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, Fox 5

item.title: DOJ fires prosecutors who worked on Trump criminal cases
item.description: Justice Department officials fired several prosecutors who worked on President Trump's criminal cases, saying they could not “trust” them.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, ABC 4

item.title: Super Bowl footballs are ready to go within hours of the matchup being set
item.description: As soon as this year's Super Bowl matchup was set, workers at the Wilson Sporting Goods football factory jumped into action. The factory in the rural village of Ada, Ohio, makes the game balls used by every NFL team along with many of the nation’s top college programs and high schools.
item.dc:creator: JOHN SEEWER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Coke jumps on nostalgic trend with new Coca-Cola Orange Cream flavor
item.description: A year after the launch of the short-lived Coca-Cola Spiced, Coke is adding another new flavor to its lineup.
item.dc:creator: Dee-Ann Durbin, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Starbucks offering free refills to all customers, bringing back condiment bar
item.description: Starbucks is implementing new and old policies at its cafes in the United States and Canada to improve the customer experience. Beginning on Monday, customers who order a “for here” beverage can also get free refills of hot or iced coffee or hot or iced tea during their visit.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: White House says Colombia will take deported migrants after Trump showdown
item.description: The White House said Sunday night that a U.S.-Colombia agreement had come together in the wake of a back-and-forth between the two countries over topics including immigration and tariffs.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, Fox 5

item.title: LIVE: Auschwitz memorial marks 80th anniversary of the death camp's liberation
item.description: Nazi German forces murdered some 1.1 million people at the site in southern Poland, which was under German occupation during World War II.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC
